# Plan Phase: AI Agent Activity Dashboard

**Product**: `agent-alchemy-dev`  
**Feature**: `agent-dashboard`  
**Phase**: Plan (Phase 2 of 5)  
**Status**: ✅ Complete  
**Generated**: 2026-03-13  
**Generated By**: Agent Alchemy Plan SKILL v2.0.0  
**Recommendation Applied**: Hybrid Supabase Realtime Dashboard (Proposal 3)

---

## Summary

This plan phase translates the research findings (FEASIBILITY-SUMMARY.md: **PROCEED** with Proposal 3) into six concrete specification artifacts covering every planning concern for the AI Agent Activity Dashboard.

The dashboard is a real-time mission-control interface for human operators overseeing autonomous AI agents. It surfaces agent status, developer-log notes, decisions, questions, GitHub work items, and session history using:

- **Supabase Realtime** for live agent activity streams
- **Angular 18+ Signals + OnPush** for reactive, performant UI
- **PrimeNG Timeline** for the activity feed
- **Supabase Edge Functions** for GitHub API proxying (zero tokens in browser)
- **`@agent-alchemy/agent-sdk`** for ergonomic agent integration

---

## Specification Artifacts

### 1. Functional Requirements
**File**: [`functional-requirements.specification.md`](./functional-requirements.specification.md)  
**Specification**: `1-functional-requirements`

Defines all 10 functional requirements (FR-001 through FR-010) with Gherkin acceptance criteria and TypeScript interface definitions:

| Requirement | Priority |
|-------------|----------|
| FR-001: Real-time Agent Status Display | Critical |
| FR-002: Agent Card Display | Critical |
| FR-003: Developer Log Feed | Critical |
| FR-004: GitHub Work Item Panel | High |
| FR-005: Multi-Agent Support (50 agents) | Critical |
| FR-006: Activity Entry Types (7 types) | Critical |
| FR-007: Session Management | High |
| FR-008: Filtering and Search | High |
| FR-009: Historical Session View | Medium |
| FR-010: Agent SDK Integration | Critical |

---

### 2. Non-Functional Requirements
**File**: [`non-functional-requirements.specification.md`](./non-functional-requirements.specification.md)  
**Specification**: `2-non-functional-requirements`

Defines quality attributes, performance targets, and system constraints:

| NFR Category | Key Target |
|-------------|------------|
| Performance | Dashboard loads < 500ms; updates visible < 3s |
| Scalability | Supabase free tier for MVP; Pro plan for 50+ agents |
| Security | Zero GitHub tokens in browser; RLS on all tables |
| Accessibility | WCAG 2.1 AA compliance; full keyboard navigation |
| Reliability | 99.5% uptime; graceful Realtime reconnection |
| Maintainability | Angular Signals only; OnPush everywhere; lazy-loaded module |
| Bundle Size | Dashboard feature chunk < 150KB gzipped |
| Browser Support | Chrome 90+, Firefox 88+, Safari 14+, Edge 90+ |

---

### 3. Business Rules
**File**: [`business-rules.specification.md`](./business-rules.specification.md)  
**Specification**: `3-business-rules`

Defines 11 business rules governing data integrity, lifecycle, and security:

| Rule | Category |
|------|----------|
| BR-001: Activity entry ownership (agentId + sessionId required) | Data Integrity |
| BR-002: Session lifecycle state machine | Lifecycle |
| BR-003: GitHub data cache TTL (5 minutes) | Caching |
| BR-004: Data retention (90 days activity, 1 year sessions) | Governance |
| BR-005: Rate limiting (100 entries/min per agent) | Throttling |
| BR-006: Multi-tenancy via Row Level Security | Security |
| BR-007: Activity entry immutability | Audit |
| BR-008: Realtime subscription org-scoped (no global subscriptions) | Security |
| BR-009: Agent identity uniqueness per org | Data Integrity |
| BR-010: Question answer protocol (answered once, immutable) | Lifecycle |
| BR-011: Heartbeat protocol (60s ping, 120s offline threshold) | Reliability |

---

### 4. UI/UX Workflows
**File**: [`ui-ux-workflows.specification.md`](./ui-ux-workflows.specification.md)  
**Specification**: `4-ui-ux-workflows`

Defines user workflows, ASCII wireframes, and component interaction specifications:

| Workflow | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| UX-001: Dashboard Overview | Two-panel layout (38/62 split), header bar, footer |
| UX-002: Agent Card Design | Status badges, project/repo display, open questions count, card states |
| UX-003: Activity Feed | PrimeNG Timeline, 7 entry types, auto-scroll, decision expansion |
| UX-004: GitHub Work Item Panel | Issue/PR cards, state badges, label chips, staleness warning |
| UX-005: Filter Sidebar | Status chips, project/repo dropdowns, entry type toggles |
| UX-006: Session History | Paginated list, session detail with summary stats |
| UX-007: Empty States | No agents, no activity, agent offline, no filter results |
| UX-008: Error States | Connection lost, GitHub unavailable, rate limited |
| UX-009: Mobile Responsive | Single-panel stack, tab navigation, touch interactions |
| UX-010: Navigation and Header | Connection indicator, live counts, session history link |

### 5. Implementation Sequence
**File**: [`implementation-sequence.specification.md`](./implementation-sequence.specification.md)  
**Specification**: `5-implementation-sequence`

Defines the 7-sprint phased delivery plan:

| Phase | Sprints | Goal | Milestone |
|-------|---------|------|-----------|
| MVP Foundation | 1–3 | Schema + SDK + Angular polling UI + GitHub | MVP Demo |
| Realtime Integration | 4 | Supabase Realtime subscriptions | Live Dashboard |
| GitHub Integration | 5 | GitHub webhooks + filters | GitHub-enriched |
| Filtering & History | 6 | Session history + unit/component tests | Full-featured |
| Polish & Production | 7 | Performance, accessibility, E2E, docs | v1.0 GA |

**Total**: 14 weeks, 1.5 engineers, $72K–$96K development cost

---

### 6. Constraints and Dependencies
**File**: [`constraints-dependencies.specification.md`](./constraints-dependencies.specification.md)  
**Specification**: `6-constraints-dependencies`

Documents all bounds on implementation:

| Category | Key Items |
|----------|-----------|
| Technical Constraints | Supabase free tier limits; GitHub API rate limits; Angular Signals mandate; PrimeNG only; no browser GitHub tokens |
| External Dependencies | `@supabase/supabase-js ^2.52.0` (existing); GitHub REST API v3; PrimeNG 18+; Tailwind CSS |
| Team Dependencies | Agent SDK publication; auth strategy decision; Supabase project provisioning; GitHub App token |
| Business Constraints | No tokens in browser; multi-tenancy required; v1.0 read-only; 90-day retention; SDK backward compatibility |
| Nx Library Boundaries | Dashboard in `apps/agent-alchemy-dev/features/dashboard/`; SDK in `libs/agent-alchemy/agent-sdk/` |
| Risk Mitigations | Memory leak prevention; single org-level channel; ETag caching; Signal batching |

---

## Key Decisions Made in This Plan Phase

### Decision 1: Architecture — Hybrid Supabase Realtime (Proposal 3)
**Decision**: Proceed with Proposal 3 per FEASIBILITY-SUMMARY.md recommendation.  
**Rationale**: Supabase already in stack; built-in Realtime; PostgreSQL persistence; no custom server; lowest TCO.  
**Impact**: All specifications are Proposal 3 aligned.

### Decision 2: Single Org-Level Realtime Channel
**Decision**: Use one Supabase Realtime channel per organization (not one per agent).  
**Rationale**: Prevents channel proliferation at 50 agents; reduces WebSocket connection overhead.  
**Impact**: Activity entry routing to per-agent Signals handled in TypeScript, not via subscription filtering.

### Decision 3: Angular Signals Only (No RxJS for UI State)
**Decision**: All reactive state in the dashboard uses Angular Signals. RxJS is only permitted as a bridge adapter for external event sources.  
**Rationale**: Consistency with `apps/agent-alchemy-dev` patterns; simpler subscription management; better performance with OnPush.  
**Impact**: `DashboardService` defines `signal<T>` for all state; no `BehaviorSubject` used.

### Decision 4: v1.0 is Read-Only
**Decision**: Human operators cannot issue commands, pause, or stop agents in v1.0.  
**Rationale**: Per FEASIBILITY-SUMMARY.md Q5 decision. Bidirectional control adds significant complexity and risk.  
**Impact**: No `agent_commands` table; no SDK receive loop; no command UI components.

### Decision 5: Service Role Key for Agent Authentication (MVP)
**Decision**: Agents use a Supabase service role key (or org-scoped anon key) for MVP.  
**Rationale**: Simpler implementation for Sprint 1; acceptable security posture for MVP.  
**Impact**: Must plan key rotation strategy before v1.0 GA (Sprint 7 security audit).

### Decision 6: 90-Day Activity Retention
**Decision**: Activity entries retained for 90 days per FEASIBILITY-SUMMARY.md Q4.  
**Rationale**: Balances storage cost vs. audit trail depth; sessions kept 1 year for longer-term audit.  
**Impact**: Nightly retention purge Edge Function; historical view bounded to 90 days for activity.

### Decision 7: Dashboard in App Feature Directory
**Decision**: Dashboard components and services live in `apps/agent-alchemy-dev/features/dashboard/` (not in a shared lib).  
**Rationale**: Dashboard is app-specific; not reusable across other apps; shared libs reserved for cross-app concerns.  
**Impact**: Agent SDK (`@agent-alchemy/agent-sdk`) in `libs/agent-alchemy/agent-sdk/` as the only shared artifact.

## Metrics to Track During Implementation

Per FEASIBILITY-SUMMARY.md success criteria:

**Performance**:
- Dashboard load time: < 500ms (measure at Sprint 3 MVP demo)
- Activity update latency: < 3s (measure at Sprint 4 Realtime milestone)
- Agent card refresh on status change: < 100ms Signal update

**Quality**:
- Service test coverage: > 80% (target by Sprint 6)
- Component test coverage: > 70% (target by Sprint 6)
- WCAG 2.1 AA violations: 0 critical/serious (target Sprint 7)

**Business**:
- SDK integration: > 90% of active agents using SDK (target post-GA)
- Blocked agent detection time: < 5 minutes (monitor post-GA)
- Open question response time: < 5 seconds to notification (measure Sprint 4)

---

## Next Steps — Architecture Phase

The Plan phase is complete. The next phase is **Architecture** (Phase 3 of 5).

**Architecture Phase Will Produce**:
1. `architecture/component-architecture.specification.md` — Angular component tree, Signal graph, module structure
2. `architecture/data-architecture.specification.md` — Complete Supabase SQL schema, indexes, RLS policies, migration scripts
3. `architecture/api-design.specification.md` — Agent SDK API contract, Edge Function endpoints, TypeScript interfaces
4. `architecture/realtime-architecture.specification.md` — Channel design, event routing, reconnection strategy
5. `architecture/security-architecture.specification.md` — RLS policy design, token management, auth flow

**Before Architecture Begins**:
- [ ] Stakeholder sign-off on Plan phase specifications
- [ ] Phase 0 pre-work initiated (Supabase project, agent auth decision)
- [ ] Sprint 1 team allocation confirmed (1 Senior Angular Dev + 0.5 Backend Dev)
